shortDescription.text: Identifies code designed to execute malicious commands or scripts.

fullDescription.text: This rule flags code snippets that appear to execute arbitrary commands, shell scripts, or other potentially harmful operations, indicating malicious intent such as system compromise or data manipulation.

shortDescription.text: Detects attempts to send sensitive data to external, unauthorized destinations.

fullDescription.text: This rule identifies patterns indicative of data exfiltration, such as network requests to suspicious domains, file uploads to unknown servers, or encoding/transfer of sensitive information outside the expected operational boundaries.

shortDescription.text: Flags code attempting to establish persistence on a system.

fullDescription.text: This rule looks for mechanisms that allow an attacker to maintain access to a system across reboots or after initial compromise, such as modifying startup scripts, creating scheduled tasks, or injecting into legitimate processes.

shortDescription.text: Identifies hardcoded sensitive credentials within the code.

fullDescription.text: This rule flags instances where API keys, passwords, tokens, or other sensitive authentication material are directly embedded in the source code, posing a significant security risk if exposed.

shortDescription.text: Identifies content that is purely informational or documentation.

fullDescription.text: This rule is used to classify content that serves as documentation, guidance, or descriptive text without containing any executable code, malicious indicators, or security vulnerabilities.
